own_review.issue: Fetch targets https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt, but disclaimer.txt does not exist on master at the base commit. Since response.ok is never checked, every pre-merge build (including the linked Vercel preview deployment) renders GitHub's '404: Not Found' body as the legal disclaimer text. The page only becomes correct after the PR itself is merged — a chicken-and-egg dependency that should be called out and verified.

No error handling on the fetch: no response.ok check, no try/catch, no fallback. A 404 or rate-limit response body is silently rendered as the disclaimer content (fetch only rejects on network errors, so this fails silently rather than crashing). Existing privacy-policy/terms pages share the flaw, but new code repeats it and it actively manifests here pre-merge.

URL uses the mutable 'master' ref. The in-repo precedent Website/app/privacy-policy/page.tsx pins a commit SHA (c86ce85731008f20ec5a67d423a5047d49ebc40d); pinning (post-merge) would make the page immune to later file moves/edits. (terms/page.tsx is also unpinned.)

Stale/contradictory description: it claims the disclaimer text 'is currently loaded from my fork' and the link must be updated after merge, but the diff already points at the main repo (SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty). Any review repeating the fork claim is echoing a fabrication; the real follow-up needed is the missing-file/404 problem, not a URL swap.
Unrelated dependency churn: @eslint/plugin-kit bumped 0.3.1 -> 0.3.4 with a nested @eslint/core 0.15.1 entry. Out of scope for a disclaimer PR; should be reverted or split out.

Unexplained layout changes beyond adding the link: flex-1 -> flex-auto changes the three-column flex balance (the nav column no longer shares space equally with the copyright/social columns), and space-x-6 -> space-x-4 tightens spacing. Plausibly intentional to fit the extra item, but undocumented in the PR.
Missing trailing newline ('\ No newline at end of file').
Disclaimer text duplicated verbatim in README.md and disclaimer.txt; the two copies can drift out of sync.

points.point: page.tsx:4 fetch URL 'points to the author's fork', PR description acknowledges post-merge update; recommend commit-pinned URL like privacy-policy; terms page also unpinned
points.note: Central premise is fabricated: SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty IS the main repo, not the author's fork — a stale-PR-description echo asserted as fact. The pinning advice and the privacy-pins/terms-doesn't facts are verified true, but the point misdiagnoses the actual problem (file absent on master => 404) as a hypothetical 'if deleted later'.

points.point: page.tsx no error handling (no response.ok, no try/catch); matches existing pages; suggest guard across all three
points.note: Real and verified. Minor inaccuracy: a 404 does not 'crash the build' — it silently renders the error body, which is worse — but the finding stands.

points.point: Disclaimer text duplicated between README and disclaimer.txt; drift risk
package-lock.json unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit 0.3.1->0.3.4 bump; revert for scope
Footer space-x-6 -> space-x-4 reasonable, no objection
points.note: Awareness-only; also omits the flex-1 -> flex-auto change, the more consequential half of the line.

missed: disclaimer.txt is absent from master at the base commit, so pre-merge builds/preview render '404: Not Found' as the disclaimer — the sharpest real issue; v5 only gestures at hypothetical future breakage

points.point: page.tsx:3-4 no error handling on external fetch; could fail at build time; recommend try/catch + pinned commit like privacy-policy
points.note: Real and verified. Slight inaccuracy ('crash the page at build time' — a 404 renders silently rather than crashing), but the finding and remediation are sound.

points.point: page.tsx:3 URL uses mutable master ref; pin commit hash matching privacy-policy/page.tsx
points.note: Verified: privacy-policy pins c86ce857...; this PR uses master. Echoes the stale 'URL will need updating after merge' line but makes no fork claim.

points.point: Footer.tsx:16 unmentioned CSS changes flex-1 -> flex-auto and space-x-6 -> space-x-4; call out or revert
points.note: Catches both class changes (v5 caught only one) and is actionable.

points.point: package-lock.json unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit + nested @eslint/core bump; separate PR
Content duplication README.md vs disclaimer.txt; keep in sync or link instead

missed: disclaimer.txt is absent from master at the base commit, so pre-merge builds/preview render '404: Not Found' as the disclaimer — v7 treats the fetch failure as hypothetical ('if the CDN is down or the file moves') rather than the current pre-merge reality

qualitative: The human reference is a bare LGTM, so both machine reviews exceed it on substance. v7 is the cleaner review: six points, five of them verified real (error handling, mutable-master vs pinned-commit precedent, both footer class changes, package-lock churn, content duplication), and zero false claims — it merely echoes the stale 'update after merge' line without asserting the fork fabrication. v5 covers similar ground but its lead point flatly states the URL 'points to the author's fork', a fabricated echo of the stale PR description (the URL already targets the main repo), and its footer point misses the flex-1->flex-auto change. Both candidates missed the sharpest verified issue: disclaimer.txt does not exist on master at the base commit, so until this PR merges the page renders GitHub's '404: Not Found' as the legal disclaimer — each framed the failure as hypothetical instead of current. Neither candidate fabricated absences; all 'no error handling / not pinned / not described' claims checked out against the repo.
